elements.sent-2411-001: Motivated by Gromov's geodesic flow problem on hyperbolic groups $G$, we develop in this paper an analog using random walks.
elements.sent-2411-002: This leads to a notion of a harmonic analog $\Theta$ of the Bowen-Margulis-Sullivan measure on $\partial^2 G$.
elements.sent-2411-003: We provide three different but related constructions of $\Theta$: 1) by moving the base-point along a quasigeodesic ray 2) by moving the base-point along random walk trajectories 3) directly as a push-forward under the boundary map to $\partial^2 G$ of a measure inherited from studying all bi-infinite random walk trajectories (with no restriction on base-point) on $G^\Z$.
elements.sent-2411-004: Of these, the third construction is the most involved and needs new techniques.
elements.sent-2411-005: It relies on developing a framework where we can treat bi-infinite random walk trajectories as analogs of bi-infinite geodesics on complete simply connected negatively curved manifolds.
elements.sent-2411-006: Geodesic flow on a hyperbolic group is typically not well-defined due to non-uniqueness of geodesics.
elements.sent-2411-007: We circumvent this problem in the random walk setup by considering all trajectories.
elements.sent-2411-008: We thus get a well-defined discrete flow given by the $\Z-$shift on bi-infinite random walk trajectories.
elements.sent-2411-009: The $\Z-$shift is the random analog of the time one map of the geodesic flow.
elements.sent-2411-010: As an analog of ergodicity of the geodesic flow on a closed negatively curved manifold, we establish ergodicity of the $G$-action on $(\partial^2G, \Theta)$.
elements.sent-2411-011: The aim of this paper is to develop a random walks analog of geodesic flow on hyperbolic groups.

elements.sent-2411-021: A key technical problem addressed in many of these papers is the fact that geodesics between pairs of points in a hyperbolic group are only coarsely unique, but not unique in general.
elements.sent-2411-022: We adopt a completely opposite point of view in this paper, by considering all possible paths, thus doing away entirely with the problem of selecting a preferred path.

elements.sent-2411-156: However, where they regard $\partial^2 G\cross \R$ as their ``space of all geodesics with all parametrizations", we look at $G^{\Z}$, the space of all random walk trajectories with all parametrizations.

elements.subsection-2411-004: Organization of the rest of the article
elements.sent-2411-164: The remaining part of the paper is organized as follows: In Section \ref{sec background , we will provide the background on hyperbolic groups needed throughout the paper.}

elements.subsection-2411-009: Construction of $\Theta_2$: Dynamics along random paths
elements.sent-2411-418: The measure $\Theta_1$ constructed in the previous section \ref{sec Theta bd depends on a specific choice of bi-infinite rays.}
